creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: TBPN, Silicon Valley's cult-favorite tech podcast, will operate independently, even as it's overseen by chief political operative Chris Lehane.
contentSnippet: TBPN, Silicon Valley's cult-favorite tech podcast, will operate independently, even as it's overseen by chief political operative Chris Lehane.

title: (No AI) Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models

title: (No AI) PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Granola’s logo](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/granola-logo.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

If you use the AI-powered note-taking app Granola, you might want to double-check your privacy settings. Though [Granola says](https://www.granola.ai/security) your notes are "private by default," it makes them viewable to anyone with a link, and also uses them for internal AI training unless you opt out.

Granola [describes itself](https://www.granola.ai/) as an "AI notepad for people in back-to-back meetings." It integrates with your calendar to capture audio from your meetings, and then uses AI to generate a bulleted list of what you've heard, which it calls a "note." You can edit the AI-generated notes, invite other collaborators to view them, and use Granola's AI assistant to ask questions about y …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/906253/granola-note-links-ai-training-psa)
contentSnippet:
If you use the AI-powered note-taking app Granola, you might want to double-check your privacy settings. Though Granola says your notes are "private by default," it makes them viewable to anyone with a link, and also uses them for internal AI training unless you opt out.
Granola describes itself as an "AI notepad for people in back-to-back meetings." It integrates with your calendar to capture audio from your meetings, and then uses AI to generate a bulleted list of what you've heard, which it calls a "note." You can edit the AI-generated notes, invite other collaborators to view them, and use Granola's AI assistant to ask questions about y …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
author: Elizabeth Lopatto
content:
![Elon Musk smiling with big green arrow and Space X rockets.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/STKB355_SPACE_X_IPO_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

To be honest, I thought Elon Musk would confidentially file for SpaceX's IPO on the 20th of this month, rather than the 1st. But maybe that just means he's moved on to other numbers, and we should all [mark our calendars for June 7th](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/6-7_meme) as an IPO date just in case.

Based on the April 1st filing, and the general length of an SEC review before the S-1 document becomes public, the earliest I am expecting a SpaceX IPO is June. (At least, assuming there is still anyone left at the SEC who wants to do their job instead of just glance at the first page of the filing, say, "Seems fine!" and then go out for a smoke break.) Of course, [this process could …](https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/filing-first-trade-inside-us-ipo-process-2026-04-01/)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/906027/elon-musk-lawsuit-ipo-spacex-tesla)

creator: Brian Barrett, Zoë Schiffer, Leah Feiger, Makena Kelly, Kate Knibbs
title: (No AI) ‘Uncanny Valley’: Iran’s Threats on US Tech, Trump’s Plans for Midterms, and Polymarket’s Pop-up Flop
dc:creator: Brian Barrett, Zoë Schiffer, Leah Feiger, Makena Kelly, Kate Knibbs
content: In this episode, we discuss Iran’s threats to target US tech firms, gear up for the midterm elections, and get a scene report from the Polymarket pop-up bar in DC.
contentSnippet: In this episode, we discuss Iran’s threats to target US tech firms, gear up for the midterm elections, and get a scene report from the Polymarket pop-up bar in DC.

creator: Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show ‘TBPN’—and Buys Itself Some Positive News
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff
content: OpenAI is acquiring TBPN, a business talk show that’s popular among Silicon Valley elites, as it continues to battle its negative public image.
contentSnippet: OpenAI is acquiring TBPN, a business talk show that’s popular among Silicon Valley elites, as it continues to battle its negative public image.
